what: Build a local web dashboard (FastAPI backend + single vanilla JS HTML file, no build step) that reads harness JSON files from the filesystem and presents them as a human-readable UI. Endpoints: GET / (serves dashboard.html), GET /api/tasks, GET /api/memory/{task_id}, GET /api/logs, GET /api/events (SSE), GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/approval, POST /api/approve/{task_id}. Four panels: pipeline progress (tasks.json as subtask chain with gate stamps), memory cards (context + decisions + validation per task), run log timeline (memory/run_log.jsonl), HITL approval (Approve button that POSTs to /api/approve/{task_id}).
approach: Single-file approach: one main.py with FastAPI app, one dashboard.html in project root served statically. Backend reads files directly from cwd. SSE endpoint uses asyncio + watchfiles or polling loop to detect tasks.json changes and stream updates. HITL approval writes human_approved:true to validation JSON and deletes memory/waiting_for_human.json sentinel. Client polls /api/tasks/{task_id}/approval every 3s. No database, no auth, no npm.
notes: Test the API layer only — no browser tests. Key test surfaces: GET /api/tasks returns parsed tasks.json; GET /api/memory/{task_id} merges context+decisions+validation; GET /api/logs returns array from run_log.jsonl; POST /api/approve/{task_id} writes human_approved:true and deletes sentinel; GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/approval reflects the flag. Use pytest with httpx TestClient (FastAPI). Dashboard HTML is not unit-tested — it is served as a static file.
